title: ATM in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Use a bank-branded ATM when you can.** A bank branch or lobby machine is usually a calmer first choice than a random standalone ATM near a nightlife street or station exit.
**Reject home-currency conversion on non-euro cards.** If the screen offers dollars, pounds, lira or another home currency, continue in euros if the ATM allows it.
**Read both fee screens.** Your own bank can charge a foreign withdrawal fee, and the German ATM operator can also show a separate machine fee.
**Carry a cash buffer, not a cash pile.** For many visitors, 40 to 80 euros per person is enough for cafes, markets, tips, toilets and small cash-only moments.
**Cancel if the screen feels unclear.** Berlin has enough ATMs that you rarely need to accept the first confusing fee or conversion screen.
